title: DEMOXYTOCIN
description: Demoxytocin is a synthetic analog of the neuropeptide oxytocin, modified by the removal of the N-terminal amino group to increase its resistance to enzymatic degradation and extend its half-life. It primarily functions as an agonist at the oxytocin receptor, facilitating uterine contractions and milk ejection while modulating central social behaviors and stress responses.

research.title: Oxytocin
research.summary: This systematic review details oxytocin's essential role in lactation and its potential calming effects, while noting that exogenous administration can impact maternal-infant bonding and birth outcomes.

research.title: The Peptide That Binds: A Systematic Review of Oxytocin and Social Behavior
research.summary: This review establishes that oxytocin administration alters social decision-making, eye contact, and social memory, framing it as a key regulator of the human social brain.

research.title: The role of oxytocin in psychiatric disorders: A review of biological and therapeutic research findings
research.summary: The research highlights oxytocin's therapeutic potential for conditions involving social dysfunction and anxiety, specifically citing its role in pair-bonding and social attachment.

research.title: Chronic oxytocin administration as a tool for investigation and treatment: A cross-disciplinary systematic review
research.summary: A comprehensive analysis showing that long-term oxytocin administration can improve adaptive physiological and behavioral functions across various clinical contexts.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report significant improvements in social anxiety and emotional warmth, though some note that the 'short-lived' nature of standard oxytocin is improved by the Demoxytocin analog.

effects.label: Half-Life:
effects.description: Demoxytocin is regarded as more effective than regular oxytocin because it resists breakdown by aminopeptidases, leading to a longer duration of action.

effects.label: Social Context:
effects.description: Effectiveness is highly dependent on environment; it works best in social settings rather than in isolation.

effects.label: Biological Variability:
effects.description: Users report varied results based on baseline endogenous oxytocin levels and receptor sensitivity.

dosage.label: Intranasal Administration:
dosage.description: Most commonly used via nasal spray to bypass the blood-brain barrier effectively.

dosage.label: Standard Range:
dosage.description: Typical doses range from 10 IU to 40 IU per application depending on individual tolerance.

dosage.label: Chronic vs Acute:
dosage.description: While acute doses are used for social events, chronic low-dose administration is sometimes used for mood regulation.

sideEffects.label: Mild Headache:
sideEffects.description: Some users report pressure or mild headaches shortly after administration.

sideEffects.label: Emotional Blunting:
sideEffects.description: A small subset of users reports feeling 'too calm' or slightly detached from negative emotions.

sideEffects.label: Uterine Contractions:
sideEffects.description: As a potent agonist, it carries a risk of stimulating uterine activity, which is a concern for pregnant users.

availability.label: Prescription Status:
availability.description: In many regions, the analog is available via prescription for lactation or labor support, though often sourced via compounding pharmacies.

availability.label: Research Chemicals:
availability.description: Frequently found in the 'nootropic' and 'peptide' grey market as a research chemical.
